migration_notes: 2026-06-13: Added additive saga_lifecycle block derived from the vendored saga-docs-model.yaml snapshot (plugin 0.21.0). At that migration, this was strictly additive and existing consumers (for example, wip_limits.olympus) were unchanged; superseded for Olympus routing by the 2026-06-17 retirement.

2026-06-13.2: Merged hand-authored config/saga-lifecycle-extras.yaml (combinations, reset_edges, gate_classification, phase_board_map, phase_command_map) into the saga_lifecycle block. Strictly additive; snapshot-derived keys and existing consumers unchanged.

2026-06-17: Retired Mount Olympus as an active board projection. phase_board_map now projects only Jeff Intent, Asgard, and CAMPPS; Project #1 is closed externally and preserved as historical context.
description: Canonical Infiquetra SDLC board, team, routing, review, approval, and deployment-state schema. This file intentionally contains no live GitHub Projects field option IDs; tooling must resolve those from GitHub at runtime.

jeff.role: Sole human operator, intent source, approval gate, escalation arbitrator, and occasional direct implementer with local AI tools

asgard.role: Jeff-proximal rapid-action, incubation, and mission-mode team with an issue-backed feed and lightweight proof model

olympus.role: Former primary autonomous engineering execution pipeline. Preserved for history only; do not route active work or current board state through Mount Olympus.

hermes.role: Single Python orchestrator process that ingests GitHub webhooks, dispatches work, coordinates plan review, and manages Discord threads

themis.role: Former PR-review state machine. Preserved for migration history only; not an active routing path.

operations.name: Operations
operations.purpose: Raw operator intent, approvals, questions, personal/operator work, and shaping before team execution

asgard.name: Asgard
asgard.purpose: Rapid action, incubation, and mission-mode work close to Jeff

campps.name: CAMPPS
campps.purpose: Portfolio-level execution board for the CAMPPS program, tracking Outcome, Capability, and Component slice progress while the initiative is active
campps.archive_trigger: Operator declares the initiative complete after Objectives are Done and Outcome Scorecards are reviewed.

work_hierarchy.source_note: Canonical machine-readable hierarchy for Objective, Outcome, Capability, and Component slice roles. Component is a dispatch role encoded by sub-issue position plus fields, not a separate issue type.

objective.supporting_artifact: Outcome Scorecard doc in the owning context-library repository

outcome.purpose: Dated proof tier for initiative-level outcomes when a long-lived initiative board exists.

capability.purpose: Coordinates the end-to-end result across services and names the verification slice that proves completion.

encoded_by: native GitHub sub-issue position under a Capability
component.purpose: Independently service-testable slice an agent can build inside one repository or bounded context.

endpoint.proof_expectation: Request and response seam proof for one endpoint vertical.

event.proof_expectation: Event emission, idempotency, state transition, and alarm or retry proof.

schema.proof_expectation: Schema invariant, compatibility, and generated artifact proof.

property.proof_expectation: Property, exhaustiveness, or state-space proof for non-enumerable behavior.

combine.proof_expectation: Cross-repository or cross-service seam proof that local green checks did not miss the end-to-end result.

component_slices.capability_close_rule: Every Capability coordinator names one enforced end-to-end verification slice before close; use combine when a cross-repo seam exists or a dedicated closeout E2E slice otherwise.

pause_states.Plan-Approved: Plan accepted but operator intentionally paused dispatch.
pause_states.Needs Review: Operator or team review needed before work can continue.
pause_states.Needs Question: Author or operator input needed; currently treated as a human-input state.

Ready: Target Team must be set before promotion out of Operations

Active: Jeff Needed must be set when work crosses an approval boundary

Committed: Outcome or Capability is admitted to the initiative portfolio
In Progress: Component slices identify their Test Strategy when applicable

team_routing.operations_ready_rule: A Ready Operations card must name one target team before moving to another board or workflow.
team_routing.asgard_issue_feed_rule: Asgard-bound work enters through Operations Target Team=Asgard, a direct issue added to Asgard, or a context/issue pack. Before Active, set Mode, target repository or surface, risk, approval state, proof path, and transfer target when a cross-team handoff is expected.
team_routing.cross_team_transfer_rule: Asgard and CAMPPS are sibling active target surfaces. Cross-team movement happens only when an operator explicitly routes, transfers, clones, or links work to another board; readiness checks may warn about target fit but must not infer movement. Mount Olympus is retired historical context and is not an active target.
team_routing.default_pressure_valve: Prefer project views over new boards unless scale, automation, or reporting needs justify a separate board.

review_model.team_owned_review: The team that owns the work owns PR review, evidence, and merge readiness. Hermes may coordinate events, but does not route active PR review to Themis.
review_model.plan_review: Hermes can coordinate plan review before dispatch.

retired.Themis: Historical PR-review state machine. Do not route active PR review to Themis from current SDLC docs.

human_approval_state.active_pattern: Per-agent direct message or per-card thread request to Jeff, depending on the work context.
approval_required_for: major team or process authority changes

fields.Deploy Target: Environment or promotion target requested by the work item
fields.Deploy State: Current deployment or promotion state derived from deployment records
fields.Snapshot Version: Immutable version or tag promoted across environments
fields.Promotion Pending: Whether a promotion approval or gate is waiting
fields.Release Risk: Operational risk classification for the release or promotion
fields.Last Deployment: Most recent deployment evidence link or timestamp

deployment_state.display_owner: mission-control may display or link deployment state in a later pass

saga_lifecycle.source_note: Generated by tools/docs/gen_saga_schema.py from the vendored saga snapshot (config/saga-docs-model.yaml) merged with the hand-authored config/saga-lifecycle-extras.yaml. Snapshot-derived: phases, state_axes, maturity derivation, gates_raw. Extras-authored: combinations, reset_edges, gate_classification, phase_board_map, phase_command_map.

gates_by_command.office-hours: Never implement, plan, scaffold, or file an SDLC issue.
gates_by_command.ideate: Preserve rejection reasons; do not treat operator seed ideas as exempt from critique.
gates_by_command.brainstorm: Keep WHAT and acceptance examples clear enough for /plan.
gates_by_command.spec: Do not produce a spec after message 1; interrogate first and read code before technical questions.
gates_by_command.plan: Does not implement or run the review gauntlet.
gates_by_command.doc-review: P0/P1 findings block /work without recorded override.
gates_by_command.work: Hard test gate by risk; hard review gate on P0/P1 or stale review; PR and merge require confirmation.
gates_by_command.code-review: P0/P1 findings block PR-ready unless overridden.
gates_by_command.qa: Verdict threshold determines ship, ship-with-deferred, or no-ship.
gates_by_command.handoff: Ask when source, maturity, target repo, team, or issue type is ambiguous.
gates_by_command.retro: Self-edit safety gate; modifications/deletions require explicit apply/skip/modify choice.
gates_by_command.resume: Read-only on world; Tier 2 session forensics only when no saga and no resolvable issue exist.
gates_by_command.investigate: No fix without root-cause chain grounded in observed evidence.
gates_by_command.optimize: Hard degenerate gates before any LLM judge; keep only real measured improvement.
gates_by_command.strategy: Interview-driven update with pushback; do not implement or file issues.
gates_by_command.loop: Enforces doc-review P0/P1 hard gate before /work; stub/off-chain routes are advisory.

main_chain.reason: Turn idea or requirements context into HOW.
Execute only after no unresolved P0/P1 review findings or recorded override.
Capture lifecycle learning when work is complete.

legal.note: any phase can be handed off
plan finished, advancing to the review gate

illegal.reason: a done thread cannot have an in-progress phase
a done thread cannot have a pending phase (status=done requires phase_status=complete)

reset_edges.reason: Accepted scope change from founder-review routes back to /plan for a re-plan that re-runs /doc-review (model: founder-review.routes_out + doc-review.routes_out include /plan).
A stale base (the plan's assumptions diverged from a moved main) forces a re-plan before continuing work (saga-spec convention, not in the model).
A failed acceptance verdict keeps lifecycle_phase=work for repair routing (model: qa.saga_state_behavior 'on fail, keeps lifecycle_phase=work'; qa.routes_out includes /work).
Blocking code-review findings route back into the work loop before PR-ready (model: code-review.routes_out includes /work).

doc-review.source: saga-docs-model.yaml commands.doc-review.gates ('P0/P1 findings block /work without recorded override')
doc-review.note: Authoritative implementation-readiness gate; unresolved P0/P1 block /work; advances review -> work; overridable only with a recorded rationale.

code-review.note: Internal quality lens at the work->PR boundary, NOT a substitute for team-owned PR review; advisory in the authority sense; does not advance the lifecycle spine.

qa.source: saga-docs-model.yaml commands.qa (saga_state_behavior: 'on pass, advances qa-track'; gates: 'Verdict threshold determines ship, ship-with-deferred, or no-ship')
qa.note: Post-merge acceptance-evidence verdict; advisory to the team-owned gate; a passing verdict advances the thread qa->retro (the qa-track is recorded), a no-ship keeps lifecycle_phase=work; ship-with-deferred is the recorded-rationale path.

plan-review.source: config/sdlc-schema.json review_model.plan_review ('Hermes can coordinate plan review before dispatch')
plan-review.note: Hermes-coordinated plan readiness before dispatch; blocks dispatch; does not itself advance the stored lifecycle_phase (/plan completing does).

team-owned-pr-review.note: The authoritative PR review + merge-readiness gate; the team or initiative surface that owns the work owns it; clearing it (with merge) advances work->qa; not unilaterally overridable (the owner decides).

review.note: The doc-review gate is the Ready->Active gate on Operations / Asgard (plan approved before work goes Active); on CAMPPS the work is Committed (admitted to the portfolio) before execution starts.

work.note: Work (with code-review at the PR boundary) runs in Active on Operations / Asgard and In Progress on CAMPPS.

qa.note: The qa verdict gates Verify -> Done on Operations / Asgard; CAMPPS has no separate verify column, so qa stays In Progress on the initiative rollup until the work is Done.

retro.note: Learn (/retro) runs post-Done; no dedicated board column.

issue_fields.source_note: Generated by tools/docs/gen_issue_contract.py from the issue_fields block in config/sdlc-schema.json (U2: full field set, faithfully extracted from card_validator.py). DATA only -- the card_validator algorithm (home-lab) is never generated (KTD2).

template.description: One sentence stating what the capability delivers.
Why this matters + the end-state (distinct from the one-line Objective), so the agent can re-derive the right action when the plan no longer applies.
Explicit list of things the agent MUST NOT do. If there are no non-goals, write "None" rather than leaving blank.
The countable inputs (files, IDs, endpoints, fixtures) confirmed to exist before step 1. Required for high-risk items.

semantic_check.name: plausible_path
semantic_check.message: no plausible file paths found (expected lines containing `/` or a file extension)

template.description: Path list, one per line. Planner verifies each parent directory exists.
File path + test function name, OR "No new tests required" with a one-line rationale.
Pre-mortem table: condition -> pre-authorized response (do-instead, not a bare don't). Required for high-risk items.
Explicit escalation triggers keyed to actions (when to stop and ask rather than proceed). Required for high-risk items.

Deep-links into the project's long-running context-library repo. Required: link the relevant context, or write `_none_` to declare no link applies. Use labeled subsections so policy-dependent reviewers know which doc applies.
Free-form. Pattern-to-follow hints, constraints, or context about why the card exists.

semantic_check.name: checklist_items
semantic_check.message: no checklist items found (expected at least one `- [ ] ...` line)

executable_check.name: executable_criteria
executable_check.message: acceptance criteria are not executable (expected each criterion to name a runnable check -- a command in a `code span` or a ``` fenced block -- with its expected result)

template.description: Testable checklist. Each item names a runnable check (command + expected result) the reviewer/agent can execute to self-verify.

semantic_check.name: fenced_code_block
semantic_check.message: no fenced code block found (expected commands inside a ``` ``` block)

template.description: Exact commands the agent runs to prove success. Shell code block.
Backpointer to the originating saga thread / plan doc. Auto-populated by the /handoff compile step; authors leave blank.

rules.rationale: Always-required core (context package R1/R3/R4): objective + intent + non_goals + files_expected + tests_required + acceptance_criteria + verification + context_library_links are required for every hermes-task issue type at every Risk. context_library_links is required-or-`_none_`.
rules.source: issue-context-package-requirements.md R1 (Intent), R3 (non-goals), R4 (context-links required-or-_none_); foundation REQUIRED_FIELDS

rules.rationale: Baseline-optional: notes is free-form; failure_modes / inputs / stop_conditions are risk-conditional (a high-blast rule below flips them on); lifecycle_origin is auto-populated by the compile step (R10), never author-required.
Risk-conditional rigor scales to blast radius (completeness != maximalism): a failure-modes pre-mortem, an inputs inventory, and stop-conditions are required at high-blast Risk (high).
rules.source: issue-context-package-requirements.md R5/R6/R7 (high-risk/high-blast); risk tiers from config/labels.json

rules.rationale: Risk-conditional rigor scales to blast radius (completeness != maximalism): a failure-modes pre-mortem, an inputs inventory, and stop-conditions are required at high-blast Risk (very-high).
rules.source: issue-context-package-requirements.md R5/R6/R7 (high-risk/high-blast); risk tiers from config/labels.json
